title: LCL in the Plug Entry
wikitext:
[]: LCL in the Plug Entry             Quote Post by leninacrowne » Thu Oct 31, 2013 12:43 pm Not sure if it bothers only me (hope not), but I have no idea how the LCL works in the entry plug and I'll explain why. The pilot enters the entry plug and it gets filled with LCL. So the pilot's cockpit-alike plug is filled with the orange liquid. But when the pilot can see outside the evangelion (I also don't know how it works, how the pilot can see outside thru the Evangelion eyes since it has no windows or anything else?) the entry plug seems empty, no more LCL in there so I thought maybe it is drained (even because if the pilot aims while inside the LCL the liquid refraction will make he miss the target) but then sometimes when the pilot is bersek or in danger, while screaming we can see bubbles which means the LCL is still in there. So how it works?

[]:             Quote Post by Rpahut » Thu Oct 31, 2013 1:27 pm Well, until someone decided to turn LCL into Litith's blood, I was sure it is an artificial liquid made to deliver sensory information, including visuals, directly to the pilot. In more sci-fi'ish manga that's exactly what LCL is, but in NGE - I don't really know any more.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Oct 31, 2013 2:01 pm Quantum Magic.

[]:             Quote Post by Noah » Thu Oct 31, 2013 3:42 pm I never had a problem with this. The plug looks empty the same way a glass of water can look empty. LCL is orange, but with the right amount and type of light that could be compensated for. The bigger problem is how sound travels. Some people use the explanation that speech goes in and out of the cockpit via neural link, not sound. But that doesn't account for how we can hear sound effects and non-pilots in the Evas. As for the perspective of the pilot's visuals, that is a separate question, I think. In episode 2, Shinji has a flashback of his battle with the Angel, and afterwards he sees Eva 01's own eyeball to the far right of his vision. And it seems to see him, too! That always bugged me. Some times the LCL acts like a liquid, sometimes like a gas. Sometimes the perspective of the pilot is from the eyes, sometimes it's from somewhere else. Yepp, quantum mechanics.

[]:             Quote Post by leninacrowne » Thu Oct 31, 2013 3:46 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Quantum Magic.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Oct 31, 2013 5:23 pm I theorize that images from the Eva's "eyes" are fed to wraparound monitors on the walls of the entry plug. As to the different color of the LCL, it changes color when it comes into contact with electric current. And Noah, I don't think the eye is looking AT Shinji. It seems to be locking itself into a neutral position, upon rewatching the scene.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Oct 31, 2013 7:02 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: I theorize that images from the Eva's "eyes" are fed to wraparound monitors on the walls of the entry plug.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Oct 31, 2013 7:47 pm I thought the Eva's cameras were wired to its brain, hence Unit 01 sprouting an eye when the head armor is damaged. It makes more sense for what the pilot sees to be projected into their head than onto some kind of weirdo display but I guess either is possible. LCL is clearly meant to be liquid at all times, but it only actually behaves like a liquid when it's time to boil Shinji or for Asuka's hair to look cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Oct 31, 2013 7:56 pm Chuckman wrote: It makes more sense for what the pilot sees to be projected into their head than onto some kind of weirdo display but I guess either is possible.

[]:             Quote Post by K40s » Thu Oct 31, 2013 11:53 pm Maybe it works as a direct-to-the-brain "enhanced reality" system, basically the cockpit interface (floating screenies and whatnot) are not physically there but the pilots can see them because they are being fed all that info as part of the neural link with the eva, if we were to actually see what is physically happening inside the entry plug we would see a metal tube with a 14yo kid submerged in a orange liquid that seems to be seeing and interacting with things that are not there, like they were hallucinating visuals and sounds, maybe one of the "L"s on LCL stands for "LSD"

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Fri Nov 01, 2013 12:12 pm I always thought that when they ionized the LCL it changed the consistency of it. Plus while you're immersed in water or any other medium entirely, you wouldn't see the light refraction since you're not at the liquid boundary. And the screens are sync'd with what the Eva sees so as long as the pilots react to what the Eva sees, the actions by the pilots can be directly mapped to the Eva regardless of light refraction.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 01, 2013 1:06 pm monitoradiation wrote: And the screens are sync'd with what the Eva sees

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Fri Nov 01, 2013 2:53 pm Reichu wrote: Only Eva-01 and Eva-03 have their eyes exposed at all, and, even then, not nearly well enough to provide the wrap-around view in the plug.
